findings.notes: Main service entrypoint for the Jellyfin server, responsible for startup and configuration handling
The StartupController is part of the Jellyfin.Api service, which is responsible for handling API endpoints during the startup wizard process.
This component is part of Emby's scheduled task system and is invoked during application startup, indicating it may be managed as a service component in the snap.
The service that hosts the StartDateComparer implementation, likely part of the Emby media server's sorting and metadata handling system.
Implies a service configuration for Jellyfin media server, likely managed via Snap with Tizen device integration
Device-specific service profile for Tizen 3 Stereo device, configuring media handling and streaming behavior
Device profile name used to identify Android device-specific media handling behavior in Jellyfin's service configuration.
Indicates a notification service behavior for application update availability, suggesting integration with a background notification system.
Test service context where this configuration is used to validate media encoding behavior with WebM streams.
Indicates that a build task is triggered before launching the application, suggesting a build dependency in the packaging process
Logging service configuration defines output sinks for console and file-based logging with structured message formatting
A specialized service profile that disables the web client via command-line argument.
A profile for API documentation access, launching to '/api-docs/swagger' with web browser opening enabled.
Port used by the live TV service for channel data access
Indicates a potential service or binary target for fuzzing, though not directly defined in Snap configuration
May be consumed by a service that validates keyframe timing during media encoding, implying a test service or validation component in the snap.
Device model identified in the configuration, indicating the type of hardware being managed
Indicates a potential fuzzing service or binary that may be part of a larger fuzzing pipeline
The FriendlyName identifies the device as an HDHomeRun DUAL, which has two tuners and supports ATSC broadcast standards.
Indicates the service is in offline mode, used to signal the application to skip live data fetching.
A service class responsible for creating and restoring system backups in Jellyfin, handling archive validation, version compatibility, and restoration logic.
The web server used by Jellyfin for handling HTTP/HTTPS requests and Unix socket binding.
The service under test, instantiated via AutoFixture with mocked dependencies to simulate runtime behavior during fuzzing.
The page uses a plugin configuration UI with dynamic loading and saving of repository URL via API calls.
Indicated via InternalsVisibleTo attribute for test access to internal types
Plugin service name used for metadata handling and album name replacement logic.
Suggests this project is a server-side implementation component of the Jellyfin application
A rendering library for handling graphics, including SVG and text shaping
